title: Tanarius roxburghii
taxonomy: Tanarius roxburghii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to parts of Asia, including China, India, and Nepal. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Tanarius, which contains several species of flowering plants. Tanarius roxburghii is a climbing plant with fragrant flowers, and its classification is used in botany to identify and differentiate it from other similar species. The family Annonaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, with many species found in tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Tanarius roxburghii is known to have various medicinal properties, with its root and leaves being used in traditional medicine for different purposes. The plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as cough and cold, as well as digestive problems. intro: Tanarius roxburghii, also known as Tanacetum roxburghii, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Himalayas and parts of India. The health benefits of Tanarius roxburghii include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Tanarius roxburghii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and essential oils, such as borneol, camphor, and borneol acetate, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Tanarius roxburghii include tea infusions, essential oil extracts, and herbal tinctures, which are used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions.
